title: Race-lap field context via Deep Sets or Set Transformer
summary: Pit decisions are relative to the whole field, not just one car: undercut pressure, same-compound clusters, and pit waves are inherently set-structured.
rationale: Pit decisions are relative to the whole field, not just one car: undercut pressure, same-compound clusters, and pit waves are inherently set-structured. Deep Sets and Set Transformer are built for permutation-invariant set inputs and can model cross-car interactions more directly than hand-built per-row aggregates.
implementation_hint: For each `(Year, Race, LapNumber)` build a set of all cars with per-car features; predict each car’s `PitNextLap` from its own embedding plus pooled field context. Start with a small Deep Sets model; if it helps, try attention-based Set Transformer. Blend OOF ranks with the tree stack rather than replacing it.
expected_effect: A more decorrelated ensemble member that captures pit-wave and relative-position effects the current row-wise models only approximate.
risk: More engineering and GPU cost; gains may vanish if field snapshots are too noisy or if chronology-safe sequence batching is mishandled.
